feature: Merge packages/agent-catalog source into packages/atlas/src/catalog and migrate all active consumers to @a5c-ai/atlas/catalog.
acceptanceCriteria: The catalog runtime source from packages/agent-catalog/src is available under packages/atlas/src/catalog with equivalent public behavior.
packages/atlas/package.json exposes @a5c-ai/atlas/catalog with working CommonJS/default runtime and TypeScript declarations.
All active runtime consumers import catalog APIs from @a5c-ai/atlas/catalog or @a5c-ai/atlas, not @a5c-ai/agent-catalog.
Root workspace metadata, TypeScript references, package-lock.json, build scripts, test scripts, CI, and release/package docs no longer treat @a5c-ai/agent-catalog as a standalone package.
packages/agent-catalog is removed after source/tests/docs that remain relevant are migrated.
The implementation is driven by failing tests first in each phase, then minimum implementation, refactor, verification, and adversarial review scoring until each phase scores at least 90/100.
consumerSurfaces: consumer mocks and package.json dependencies that mention @a5c-ai/agent-catalog

phases.title: Move catalog source into Atlas and expose @a5c-ai/atlas/catalog
phases.objective: Relocate agent-catalog runtime source and contract tests under packages/atlas/src/catalog while preserving the public API surface.
redTests: A package export contract test imports/requires @a5c-ai/atlas/catalog and fails before the export exists.
Moved catalog contract tests target packages/atlas/src/catalog and fail until source is present and relative imports resolve.
greenWork: Move catalog runtime source, index, bridge, cli/ui helpers, and catalog tests into packages/atlas/src/catalog.
Add packages/atlas/package.json ./catalog export with runtime and type declaration targets.
Update local catalog imports to avoid a package dependency cycle.
verification: Targeted atlas catalog contract tests pass.

phases.title: Migrate runtime consumers to @a5c-ai/atlas/catalog
phases.objective: Replace downstream @a5c-ai/agent-catalog imports and package dependencies with @a5c-ai/atlas/catalog or @a5c-ai/atlas.
redTests: Consumer contract tests fail if any runtime package still imports @a5c-ai/agent-catalog.
Vitest alias/config tests fail until aliases point to the atlas catalog surface.
greenWork: Update sdk, agent-mux adapters, hooks-mux adapters, webui/agent-platform aliases, mocks, and package dependencies.
Use @a5c-ai/atlas/catalog for catalog APIs and @a5c-ai/atlas only for root graph APIs.
verification: No non-historical runtime imports of @a5c-ai/agent-catalog remain under packages.

phases.title: Remove standalone agent-catalog workspace metadata
phases.objective: Make atlas the only package and update build order, workspace metadata, project references, CI/release docs, and package-lock entries.
redTests: A metadata guard fails while workspace/package/lockfile/CI surfaces still treat packages/agent-catalog as a package.
Build-order tests fail until build:sdk and related scripts no longer build @a5c-ai/agent-catalog.
greenWork: Remove agent-catalog from root scripts, lockfile workspace package entries, TypeScript references, release/CI surfaces, and package docs.
Rename or update catalog contract test scripts to validate atlas/catalog.
verification: npm run build:sdk, npm run test:sdk, and npm run verify:metadata pass.

phases.title: Remove packages/agent-catalog and update docs
phases.objective: Delete the standalone package after all source, tests, consumers, and metadata have moved, while keeping intentional historical references clear.
redTests: A final guard fails while packages/agent-catalog exists or active docs direct users to import @a5c-ai/agent-catalog.
A documentation/package-map check fails until public package docs describe atlas/catalog as the catalog surface.
greenWork: Remove packages/agent-catalog after preserving relevant README/policy information in atlas docs.
Update unification docs, package maps, workspace validation, release pipeline references, and atlas graph package/CI surfaces as needed.
verification: Full stale-reference guard passes for active package/runtime references.
Final acceptance commands pass and adversarial review score is at least 90.

dimensions: Spec coverage and traceability to issue #839 and docs/atlas-catalog-unification/README.md
Red/green/refactor discipline with failing tests proven before implementation
Compatibility of @a5c-ai/atlas/catalog runtime and type exports
Completeness of consumer import and package dependency migration
Workspace, lockfile, CI, docs, and atlas graph metadata cleanup
Verification command evidence and stale-reference guard coverage
